chapter: Chapter 8: Measurement of Time and Motion

questions.question: What is motion?
questions.answer: Motion is the change in position of an object with respect to time.

questions.question: What is rest in physics?
questions.answer: An object is said to be at rest if its position does not change with respect to its surroundings over time.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of time?
questions.answer: The SI unit of time is second (s).

questions.question: Name an instrument used to measure time accurately.
questions.answer: A stopwatch is used to measure time accurately.

questions.question: What is distance?
questions.answer: Distance is the total length of the path travelled by an object.

questions.question: What is speed?
questions.answer: Speed is the distance travelled by an object per unit time.

questions.question: Write the formula for speed.
questions.answer: Speed = Distance ÷ Time.

questions.question: What is uniform motion?
questions.answer: Uniform motion occurs when an object travels equal distances in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: What is non-uniform motion?
questions.answer: Non-uniform motion occurs when an object travels unequal distances in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: Name the device used to measure the speed of a vehicle.
questions.answer: A speedometer measures the speed of a vehicle.

questions.question: What does an odometer measure?
questions.answer: An odometer measures the total distance travelled by a vehicle.

questions.question: What is periodic motion?
questions.answer: Periodic motion is motion that repeats itself at regular intervals of time.

questions.question: Give one example of periodic motion.
questions.answer: The motion of a pendulum is an example of periodic motion.

questions.question: What is circular motion?
questions.answer: Circular motion is the motion of an object along a circular path.

questions.question: Give one example of circular motion.
questions.answer: The motion of the hands of a clock is an example of circular motion.

questions.question: What is oscillatory motion?
questions.answer: Oscillatory motion is the motion of an object back and forth about a fixed position.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of speed?
questions.answer: The SI unit of speed is metre per second (m/s).

questions.question: What type of motion does a freely falling object have?
questions.answer: A freely falling object has accelerated motion.

questions.question: Why is time measurement important in studying motion?
questions.answer: Time measurement helps determine speed and analyze how objects move.

questions.question: What is a simple pendulum?
questions.answer: A simple pendulum consists of a small bob suspended by a string that swings back and forth.

questions.question: What is meant by time period?
questions.answer: Time period is the time taken to complete one full cycle of periodic motion.

questions.question: What kind of motion does the Earth have around the Sun?
questions.answer: The Earth moves around the Sun in circular motion.

questions.question: What does a distance–time graph represent?
questions.answer: A distance–time graph shows how the distance travelled by an object changes with time.

questions.question: What does a straight line on a distance–time graph indicate?
questions.answer: A straight line indicates uniform motion.

questions.question: What does a curved line on a distance–time graph indicate?
questions.answer: A curved line indicates non-uniform motion.
